Title: CSN improves seed vigor of aged sunflower seeds by regulating the fatty acid, glycometabolism, and abscisic acid metabolism

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher HaLIPG1 expression is associated with enhanced triacylglycerol hydrolysis and increased soluble sugar production during germination of aged seeds after CSN treatment.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAT reduced HaLIPG1 transcript abundance during seed imbibition. 2. CSN significantly up-regulated HaLIPG1 in aged sunflower seeds. 3. This change paralleled increased lipase activity, fatty acid content, soluble sugars, germination and seedling emergence.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; enzyme activity assay for triacylglycerol lipase; metabolite profiling; seed germination and seedling emergence phenotyping
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: up-regulated in AAT+CSN seeds during imbibition
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: accelerated aging test; CSN treatment at 25 mg·L^-1 during imbibition
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate marker/gene associated with improving lipid mobilization and vigor recovery in aged sunflower seeds.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common_Gene because the paper measures transcript association and enzyme activity but does not provide direct gene-specific functional manipulation.

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation of HaLIPG3 is associated with enhanced TAG breakdown that supports sugar production and vigor restoration in aged seeds.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HaLIPG3 expression decreased after aging. 2. CSN significantly increased HaLIPG3 expression during imbibition. 3. Its expression trend matched increased lipase activity and improved germination traits.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; enzyme activity assay; metabolite profiling; germination phenotyping
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: up-regulated in AAT+CSN seeds during imbibition
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: accelerated aging test; CSN treatment at 25 mg·L^-1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate for vigor improvement strategies targeting lipid remobilization in aged oilseeds.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene-specific causality inferred from coordinated transcript/enzyme/metabolite changes, not direct mutant evidence.

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CSN-induced HaGPDH1 up-regulation is associated with improved glycerol conversion during reserve mobilization, contributing to sugar accumulation and seed vigor.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAT suppressed HaGPDH1 expression. 2. CSN significantly enhanced HaGPDH1 transcript level in aged seeds. 3. Increased GPDH activity coincided with enhanced soluble sugars and vigor.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; GPDH enzyme activity assay; sugar quantification; germination phenotyping
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for enhancing carbon flow from lipid reserves to sugars in aged sunflower seeds.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated as Common_Gene due to lack of direct gene manipulation in this study.

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HaGPDH3 expression positively correlates with improved glycometabolism and vigor in CSN-treated aged seeds.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HaGPDH3 was down-regulated by AAT. 2. CSN strongly up-regulated HaGPDH3 during imbibition. 3. This response paralleled higher GPDH activity and soluble sugar accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; enzyme activity assay; sugar profiling; germination assay
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering reserve mobilization in aged seeds.

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation of HaPCK2 is associated with increased gluconeogenic conversion supporting sugar accumulation and germination of aged seeds under CSN treatment.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAT reduced PCK activity and HaPCK2 expression. 2. CSN increased both HaPCK2 transcript level and PCK activity. 3. The paper notes PCK as a rate-limiting step in the pathway linking reserve mobilization to sugar production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; PCK enzyme activity assay; sugar quantification; germination assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced in AAT+CSN seeds during imbibition
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value candidate for vigor restoration because it may control gluconeogenic flux from lipid reserves.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although enzymatic role is pathway-related, gene-specific validation is indirect in this paper, so kept in Common_Genes.

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CSN-induced HaINV1 up-regulation is associated with increased sugar metabolism and improved germination vigor in aged seeds.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAT decreased HaINV1 expression and INV activity. 2. CSN increased HaINV1 expression in aged seeds. 3. This corresponded to increased sucrose/fructose levels and improved vigor.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; invertase enzyme activity assay; sugar quantification; phenotyping
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced by CSN in aged seeds
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate marker for monitoring sugar mobilization capacity in seed vigor improvement.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct transgenic or mutant validation.

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HaINV2 expression was reduced by aging; its role in CSN response appears weaker or not clearly significant compared with other INV paralogs.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HaINV2 transcript level decreased after AAT. 2. The paper emphasizes stronger CSN induction for HaINV1 and HaINV3, not clearly for HaINV2. 3. Included because it was among the core assayed metabolism genes in Results.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; invertase activity assay; sugar profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: down-regulated by AAT; CSN effect unclear/NA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower priority candidate than HaINV1/HaINV3 for seed vigor engineering.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CSN-induced significant up-regulation was not explicitly stated for HaINV2.

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HaINV3 positively associates with increased sugar metabolism and improved vigor in CSN-treated aged sunflower seeds.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAT suppressed HaINV3 expression. 2. CSN markedly increased HaINV3 transcript abundance. 3. This matched increased INV activity and higher soluble sugar levels in aged seeds.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; invertase activity assay; metabolite profiling; germination assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: up-regulated by CSN in aged seeds
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve sugar availability during early germination of aged seeds.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is correlative but consistent with enzyme and metabolite data.

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher HaAAO expression is associated with increased ABA accumulation in aged seeds; CSN suppresses HaAAO expression and lowers ABA content.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAT increased HaAAO transcript abundance and ABA content. 2. CSN significantly decreased HaAAO expression in aged seeds. 3. Reduced ABA correlated with improved germination and emergence.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ABA quantification; AAO enzyme activity assay; germination assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed during seed imbibition under aging conditions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher in AAT seeds, reduced by CSN
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for reducing ABA-mediated inhibition in aged seed germination.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AAO activity differences under CSN were less emphasized than transcript changes, but gene was clearly assayed and discussed as part of ABA metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: fatty acid β-oxidation and reserve mobilization linked to gluconeogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: seed storage lipid catabolism / β-oxidation module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first oxidation step of peroxisomal fatty acid β-oxidation, converting acyl-CoA to trans-2-enoyl-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CSN up-regulates HaACX3 expression and ACX activity, supporting fatty acid breakdown and downstream sugar production in aged seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAT lowered HaACX3 expression and ACX activity. 2. CSN significantly increased HaACX3 transcript level and ACX activity during imbibition. 3. This was associated with improved fatty acid utilization, soluble sugars, and seed vigor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ACX enzyme activity assay; fatty acid and sugar profiling; germination phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway engineering target for improving lipid reserve mobilization in aged oilseeds.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports energetic metabolism required for seed germination and seedling emergence.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene-specific function inferred from known enzyme assignment plus study expression/activity data.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: carotenoid cleavage step of ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HaNCED1 promotes ABA accumulation in aged seeds; CSN suppresses HaNCED1 expression, thereby contributing to lower ABA and better germination.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAT increased HaNCED1 expression and ABA content. 2. CSN significantly decreased HaNCED1 transcript level and NCED activity. 3. Reduced ABA correlated with improved aged-seed germination and emergence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; NCED enzyme activity assay; ABA quantification; germination phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher in AAT seeds and repressed by CSN
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for editing or marker-assisted selection to reduce ABA-mediated inhibition of germination in aged seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA promotes dormancy and suppresses seed germination.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper highlights NCED as one of the principal ABA-metabolism enzymes responsive to CSN.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: carotenoid cleavage step of ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HaNCED2 expression increases with aging and is associated with elevated ABA accumulation in aged seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAT increased HaNCED2 expression. 2. CSN-responsive suppression was not explicitly emphasized for HaNCED2 compared with HaNCED1/3. 3. Included because it was a core assayed ABA-pathway gene in Results.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; NCED activity assay; ABA quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: aging-induced; CSN effect not clearly stated
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential but lower-priority ABA pathway target than HaNCED1/3 based on this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CSN effect on HaNCED2 was not explicitly stated as significant.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: carotenoid cleavage step of ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HaNCED3 positively contributes to ABA accumulation in aged seeds; CSN down-regulates HaNCED3 and lowers ABA, improving germination.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAT increased HaNCED3 expression. 2. CSN significantly decreased HaNCED3 expression and NCED activity. 3. Lower ABA content under CSN was associated with improved vigor of aged sunflower seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; NCED activity assay; ABA quantification; germination assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: repressed by CSN in AAT seeds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to limit excess ABA accumulation during storage-related vigor loss.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA suppresses germination and promotes dormancy-like inhibition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Together with HaNCED1, identified in the conclusion as a major CSN-regulated ABA biosynthetic gene.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ABA at the 8' position, initiating ABA catabolism and inactivation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HaABA8ox1 promotes ABA breakdown; its induction by CSN contributes to lower ABA content and improved germination of aged seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAT down-regulated HaABA8ox1 and reduced ABA8ox activity. 2. CSN significantly up-regulated HaABA8ox1 and increased ABA8ox activity. 3. This corresponded to lower ABA accumulation and better seed vigor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ABA8ox activity assay; ABA quantification; germination assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: up-regulated by CSN in AAT seeds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable target for promoting ABA catabolism to restore aged seed vigor.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA inhibits germination; catabolism relieves inhibition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted in Results and Conclusions as one of the principal ABA-metabolism genes modulated by CSN.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid sunflower seeds from parental inbreds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ABA to initiate its catabolic inactivation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HaABA8ox3 is positively associated with ABA degradation; CSN-induced up-regulation of HaABA8ox3 helps reduce ABA in aged sunflower seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAT reduced HaABA8ox3 expression. 2. CSN significantly increased HaABA8ox3 transcript level and ABA8ox activity. 3. The resulting ABA decrease coincided with improved germination and emergence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ABA8ox activity assay; ABA quantification; germination phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced by CSN in AAT seeds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target for stimulating ABA catabolism in stored seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Relief of ABA-mediated inhibition facilitates germination.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Explicitly stated as significantly up-regulated by CSN.
